meta.title: Multi-Step Form Architecture
meta.excerpt: Building scalable multi-step forms with shared components, Zustand state management, and 41% code reuse across three different form flows

props.body: Building one multi-step form is straightforward. Building three that share code while maintaining flexibility? That requires careful architecture. Here is how we built a scalable multi-step form system.

props.text: Our application needed to support three different form flows with varying complexity. The naive approach - building three separate forms - would lead to massive code duplication and inconsistent user experiences.

features.title: Service Request
features.description: 5 steps: Basic flow for requesting electrical services with urgency levels.

features.title: Contact Form
features.description: 5 steps: General inquiries with reference linking to existing requests.

features.title: Quotation
features.description: 7 steps: Complex flow with budget ranges, timelines, and specifications.

features.title: Store Injection Pattern
features.description: Shared steps accept a store hook as a prop, allowing them to work with any Zustand store.

features.title: Container Orchestration
features.description: Each form has a container that manages step rendering, navigation, and submission.

features.title: Per-Step Validation
features.description: Each step validates its own fields before allowing navigation to the next step.

features.title: Persistent State
features.description: Zustand's persist middleware saves progress to localStorage automatically.

props.text: We identified steps that appear in multiple forms and made them truly reusable by accepting a generic store interface.
Each form has its own Zustand store that manages step navigation, form data, and persistence. The stores share a common interface that shared steps can rely on.

props.title: Why Zustand Over Context?
props.body: Zustand provides better performance (components only re-render when their specific slice changes), built-in persistence middleware, and a simpler API than React Context with useReducer.

props.text: Each form has a container component that orchestrates step rendering based on current state. The container owns the step mapping and handles the submission logic.

steps.title: Validate
steps.description: Run Zod schema on current step

props.text: The final review step triggers submission through a Server Action. The action runs security checks, validates the complete form, and sends confirmation emails.

notIdealFor: Forms where context matters across fields

props.body: Identify shared patterns, extract them into reusable components, and compose unique flows from common pieces. Our multi-step form system handles 3 different flows with 41% code reuse. The store injection pattern lets shared steps work with any form while maintaining type safety throughout.
